206568.explanation: The CDA aims to keep the property tax (ozb) affordable and not increase it. Aalten already has a low property tax rate compared to other municipalities.

206571.explanation: The CDA believes the current shop opening hours meet the needs of retailers and visitors.

206572.explanation: Because opening a store or campsite at an agricultural business contributes to the vitality of the countryside. The CDA believes this should involve own or related products.

206573.explanation: The CDA finds good integration important. This promotes mutual understanding and helps newcomers find work faster. If more funding is needed for this, the CDA is open to it. Currently, it does not seem necessary.

206574.explanation: The CDA opposes banning the use of fireplaces and wood stoves. However, it is important to have clear rules on when their use is allowed. The CDA wants to ensure people do not suffer health problems or nuisance from their use.

206575.explanation: The CDA supports solar panels on roofs.

206576.explanation: The CDA believes the municipality should be positive toward initiatives for housing construction in the municipality. Allowing farmers to build homes on their own land is part of this.

206577.explanation: The CDA keeps the option open for building a new swimming pool in the Aalten municipality. If it turns out new construction is better, the municipality should consider building a new pool.

206579.explanation: The CDA believes the municipality should always be willing to support and assist associations. This support can take various forms, including financial.

206580.explanation: Safety is more than just appointing extra enforcement officers (boa’s). The CDA believes we should especially continue focusing on prevention and intensive cooperation between different parties.

206581.explanation: The CDA recognizes the value of low-threshold contact between citizens and police. A consultation hour with the community officer can be a good way to achieve this.

206582.explanation: The CDA supports giving priority to residents of the Aalten municipality when allocating social rental housing.

206583.explanation: The CDA supports keeping the housing market accessible for everyone. The CDA follows the agreements from the regional housing agenda. 28% social rental housing in new builds is the minimum.

206584.explanation: The CDA believes this must fit within the current environment. Some restraint in allowing high residential buildings is important.

206585.explanation: The CDA focuses on building as many regular homes as possible. The CDA believes holiday homes are best suited in recreational areas.

206588.explanation: Accessibility is important to keep shopping attractive in Aalten. In the Achterhoek, the car is an important means of transport. Therefore, the CDA considers it important that shopping areas in the municipality of Aalten remain accessible by car.

206589.explanation: The CDA supports freezing the number of parking spaces designated as charging points. Currently, the demand is being met. If in the future more charging points become necessary, the CDA is open to discussing this.

206590.explanation: The CDA considers road safety within built-up areas important. Lowering the maximum speed limit for the majority of roads certainly contributes to this. Deviations can be made occasionally.

206591.explanation: The CDA believes in supporting entrepreneurs. For viable businesses, it is crucial that the entrepreneur can retain their company.

206593.explanation: The CDA attaches great importance to citizen participation. Collecting advice from residents can help in making broadly supported decisions. The municipality already has sufficient instruments for this.

206595.explanation: The CDA finds it important that the municipality of Aalten has an attractive business climate for entrepreneurs. We support an active policy to prevent vacancies. Rental measures can be a good tool for this.

206596.explanation: The CDA considers procurement from social enterprises a good way to support social entrepreneurship.

206600.explanation: The CDA sees language proficiency of labor migrants as the responsibility of the employer.

206601.explanation: Offering a free bicycle lesson could be part of the integration process for newcomers.

206602.explanation: The CDA finds it important that environmental regulations are complied with. We have no reason to believe that inspections are currently not being properly carried out.

206603.explanation: The CDA believes that festivals like these have great social value. Therefore, the municipality should simplify permit procedures. In some cases, a notification requirement might also be sufficient.

206604.explanation: The CDA welcomes new initiatives. We want to make applying for event permits as easy as possible for everyone. It is good to accommodate young people and to think along with them.

206605.explanation: The CDA finds it unfair to reject a plan for a large holiday park outright. The CDA wants to stimulate recreation and tourism. Of course, such a holiday park must be appropriate.

206606.explanation: The CDA values sustainability. With this plan, people with lower incomes also gain access to the benefits of sustainability.

206607.explanation: When too little demolition occurs, the CDA believes the red-for-red scheme should be expanded. This also creates more housing.

206608.explanation: The CDA believes that current regulations sufficiently protect residents.

206609.explanation: The CDA thinks that the owners of De Hagewind should make the decision themselves about replacing wind turbines.

206610.explanation: The CDA does not exclude the construction of new school buildings outright. Both renovating and building new schools can be necessary.

206611.explanation: The CDA sees advantages in bringing facilities together. The CDA believes the municipality should support social initiatives for this.

206612.explanation: The CDA is in favor of improving the skatepark. This should be done together with students. Make it a great social initiative.

206613.explanation: Cultural and sports associations play an important role within the municipality. Maintaining one’s own building involves risks for associations. The CDA stands for strong associations and therefore agrees with this statement.

206614.explanation: The CDA recognizes the importance of local cells (detention cells). That is why municipalities in the Achterhoek have also requested to reverse the closure of the cell complex in Doetinchem.

206615.explanation: The CDA believes owners and users are responsible for installing smoke detectors and fire extinguishers in sheds and cabins. However, the CDA believes the municipality should provide good information about fire safety.

206616.explanation: The CDA does not see the need for the municipality to establish its own housing company. The CDA finds it important that the municipality makes good agreements with housing corporations about building and renting homes to residents of Aalten.

206617.explanation: The CDA believes new homes must not cause excessive parking pressure on the street or neighborhood.

206618.explanation: Building housing for seniors is good for housing flow. This frees up homes for starters or movers. The CDA also believes the municipality should cooperate when requests come from only young people or a mix of young and older people.

206619.explanation: The CDA hopes the municipality can make agreements with local entrepreneurs or community centers regarding this. If that does not succeed, the CDA believes a public toilet is a good option.

206620.explanation: The CDA believes that homes should be vacant as little as possible but questions whether fines help against this.

206621.explanation: The CDA finds it important that the municipality cooperates in converting empty retail properties into housing. These must be retail properties outside the core shopping area. The investments involved are the responsibility of the property owners.

206623.explanation: It is good for Aalten that tourists come to the municipality. For the CDA, it is fine if the municipality promotes this by increasing advertising.

206624.explanation: Young people must learn to manage money. Therefore, the CDA considers it important that this topic receives attention. The municipality can collaborate with secondary schools on this and possibly provide resources.

206625.explanation: The CDA finds a safe crossing at this location very important. Because the N318 is a provincial road, building a bicycle tunnel is the responsibility of the province.

206626.explanation: All roads must be safe for all users. According to the CDA, the Haartseweg meets the legally prescribed requirements regarding width, layout, and road markings.

206627.explanation: The CDA believes the municipality should help addicted young people. A youth worker can assist with this. This is not the only method. It can also be done by deploying appropriate youth care or focusing more on prevention.

206628.explanation: The CDA finds it a personal choice to attend relationship therapy. Where people want to go but cannot afford it, the municipality already provides support.

206629.explanation: The CDA sees identifying and reducing loneliness as an important task and always keeps it in focus.

206630.explanation: The CDA considers it important that shops in the centers of the municipality of Aalten remain easily accessible by car. Parking spaces in the shopping center should remain.

206568.explanation: The municipality of Aalten already has a low property tax (ozb) rate.

206571.explanation: GemeenteBelangen is a co-initiator of Sunday opening. Leave the choice to open on Sundays to the entrepreneur.

206572.explanation: Give the entrepreneur (farmer) space as far as it fits within the established municipal policy.

206573.explanation: We are already using the funds we receive for this. We consider that sufficient.

206574.explanation: We have a heating guide for this purpose.

206575.explanation: Preference for roofs and lands that are unsuitable for agriculture.

206576.explanation: No uncontrolled growth. Possibilities exist through an environmental permit.

206577.explanation: A swimming facility must remain. Research must determine where and how.

206579.explanation: Provided there is consultation about the duration of the support.

206581.explanation: This also strengthens contact with the residents.

206582.explanation: For us, it may well be more than 50%.

206583.explanation: We find the current standard of 30% sufficient.

206584.explanation: Does not fit the village character.

206585.explanation: Legally it may be allowed up to 10 years. The municipality can make the assessment for this.

206588.explanation: It must remain possible to reach and park in the center.

206589.explanation: We await developments in the use of electric cars. We can always respond to the growth of the electric vehicle fleet.

206590.explanation: Fits within the recently adopted mobility policy plan. However, emphasis should be placed here on enforcement concerning other traffic.

206591.explanation: The municipality can provide tailor-made solutions in exceptional cases.

206593.explanation: The municipal council is democratically elected and knows what is happening in society.

206595.explanation: There is work to be done. Paying a year's rent goes too far for us. The starters' loan can be used.

206596.explanation: Who determines that a business is social and what does 'as much as possible' mean? A commercial entrepreneur can also implement a social policy within their organization.

206600.explanation: This is clearly a task for the entrepreneur.

206601.explanation: Here a role could lie for social institutions.

206602.explanation: Compliance with environmental regulations lies with the Environmental Service Achterhoek (ODA). Inspections take place sufficiently for us.

206603.explanation: Some form of application/notification remains necessary. Think of safety.

206604.explanation: Events remain subject to notification requirements, where the municipality can play a supportive role.

206605.explanation: We see possibilities to allow this unless there are compelling reasons not to.

206606.explanation: There are sufficient subsidy possibilities available for this.

206607.explanation: Other possibilities lie here within the framework of Vacant Agricultural Buildings.

206608.explanation: National and European regulations apply to this.

206609.explanation: This lies with the owners (general partnership) of Hagewind.

206611.explanation: Where there are opportunities for this, respond to them as much as possible.

206612.explanation: First wait for developments around the location.

206613.explanation: Associations facing financial problems can consult with the municipality.

206614.explanation: Transport to cells takes too much time and compromises regular police duties. Doetinchem is acceptable for us.

206615.explanation: Parents/owners are responsible for this. The municipality can provide information.

206616.explanation: Housing construction is not a core task of the municipality.

206617.explanation: There may be situations where this is not feasible and other solutions must be chosen.

206618.explanation: This applies if they also want to buy the land at the applicable market price.

206620.explanation: Ownership rights do not lie with the municipality. The municipality can play a mediating role.

206623.explanation: More tourism is good for Aalten. Make more use of 'Achterhoek tourism' and the municipal webpages.

206624.explanation: This is a task for schools.

206625.explanation: Provided the municipal contribution is proportional to a provincial contribution.

206628.explanation: This is not a municipal task.

206629.explanation: The municipality offers sufficient opportunities to help residents out of loneliness. It is up to the resident to make use of this.

206630.explanation: It must remain possible for people with limited mobility to reach the center and park there.

206568.explanation: Aalten already has a very low property tax (ozb). However, we must closely monitor the College to prevent unnecessary expenses.

206571.explanation: Let shops freely choose when they want to open. However, ensure a personnel policy where not all employees have to work every Sunday. This responsibility lies with the owners and branch managers.

206572.explanation: This gives farming families more freedom as entrepreneurs. It also fits perfectly with our policy of promoting small-scale tourism in Aalten.

206573.explanation: The municipality must comply with national regulations. There is a role here both for the municipality and the business community.

206574.explanation: Wood stoves are widely used in rural areas. Since distances between houses are generally large there, nuisance is limited. In the centers, people must be more considerate of each other. Burning should be allowed on days when there is no national burning ban.

206575.explanation: As long as there are still roofs to be filled and locations that are not agricultural land can be found, those locations are preferred.

206576.explanation: BBB-HMV has long advocated that a second agricultural residence should be possible. There is also often space for a care home.

206577.explanation: Why always focus on new construction when renovating the existing pool 't Walfort will probably be cheaper?

206579.explanation: Through smart arrangements, the municipality can already help sports clubs a lot. Sport is important for the social cohesion of the community.

206580.explanation: Borough officers (Boa's) do useful work. But if citizens feel unsafe, it is often due to loitering youths. Then a different kind of supervision may be more effective.

206581.explanation: We consider this useful to strengthen the community feeling in the local centers.

206582.explanation: We are facing a housing crisis. Therefore, housing our own youth is a priority.

206583.explanation: This strongly depends on demand within the municipality, which has not yet been mapped. The housing association and municipality also have responsibilities regarding the maintenance of lower-segment homes, which are often old and poorly maintained. New construction alone is not the right answer.

206584.explanation: Some high-rise buildings are allowed in a circle around the old centers.

206585.explanation: The College still does not realize that there is a housing crisis: ALL vacancies and temporary housing must be deployable.

206588.explanation: The shopping areas, as well as the hospitality areas, must be attractive. Car-restricted zones are fine, but policies must not hinder people with disabilities.

206589.explanation: At this moment, an electric car is still a luxury product, and many Aalten residents can charge electric cars on their own property. When electric cars become affordable in the future for people with a more limited budget, including tourists, a public charging infrastructure will make more sense.

206590.explanation: BBB-HMV indeed supports reducing the speed limit in the centers to 30 km/h. Alongside this, residential zones should be created around primary schools where pedestrians have priority and motorists are guests.

206591.explanation: The municipality of Aalten has a large number of self-employed persons. Support during tough times is part of the social fabric, as it keeps people out of welfare.

206593.explanation: Currently, citizen assemblies and referenda do not have a solid foundation. The question is also whether a citizen assembly promotes political engagement among the population. BBB-HMV prefers a more active role for (local) parties to engage in dialogue with citizens.

206595.explanation: All initiatives to increase offerings in the centers are useful. But the municipality should also encourage low-threshold initiatives like pop-up shops.

206596.explanation: BBB-HMV supports purchasing from local businesses: short lines and support for local initiatives. That is more important than distant social buying.

206600.explanation: The municipality must follow national policy. BBB-HMV also sees a role here for employers.

206601.explanation: Is this a role for the municipality? Perhaps the shelter location can organize something with volunteers?

206602.explanation: Environmental regulations must be enforced. This task is carried out by the Achterhoek Environmental Service (ODA).

206603.explanation: Festivals are the lubricant of society and contribute greatly to the important community spirit. However, the municipality must supervise and fairly balance the interests of individual citizens and the groups organizing the parties.

206604.explanation: BBB-HMV supports a stronger integrated youth policy. This could fit into that.

206605.explanation: The municipality of Aalten focuses on small-scale tourist facilities. This fits better in our rural area.

206606.explanation: Residents with limited means often live in older, poorly insulated homes. However, the question is whether the municipality should set up an organization for this. It may be more effective to give vouchers to those eligible for aid to purchase materials at the local hardware store.

206607.explanation: This could fit into a policy to reduce the number of ugly old buildings in rural areas.

206608.explanation: Crop protection is mainly applied in rural areas. Perhaps we should avoid difficult-to-enforce symbolic politics here?

206609.explanation: Hagenwind is currently our only wind farm. To comply with the Regional Energy Strategy, fewer but stronger turbines could be an option. It should also be considered whether individual wind turbines can be installed on industrial estates.

206610.explanation: This applies especially to our secondary schools. Concentrating schools near the station is not feasible from a zoning perspective. The building on Slingelaan needs partial renovation, but as an important village architectural feature, it is valuable.

206611.explanation: Why? Each of the three centers must retain their own sports complex. A decentralized approach is also better for accessibility.

206612.explanation: Should be integrated into a comprehensive policy for youth and young adults.

206613.explanation: Subsidizing on one hand and taxing on the other is odd and only increases administrative burden.

206614.explanation: The Doetinchem cell complex must be reinstated. It is strange that so much time is currently spent accommodating detainees outside the region.

206615.explanation: Should be integrated into a comprehensive policy for youth and young adults.

206616.explanation: Social housing is hardly profitable. Performance agreements with the housing association must be honored. The municipality should take the lead, but having its own role as a landlord may be less obvious.

206617.explanation: For now, cars remain indispensable for transport demand, especially in a rural municipality where public transport is not profitable. The question is whether parking spaces should be close to one’s home or if more parking spaces further away would be a better solution.

206618.explanation: Living together in courtyards also benefits social cohesion and responds to the increasing demand for informal care.

206619.explanation: Currently, people are referred to toilets in cafes and restaurants, but these are not always available. Well-maintained restrooms in shopping centers, like those already at the station, would be a useful addition to the existing facilities.

206620.explanation: Is this a common problem? When thinking about vacancy, perhaps we should consider doing something for youth in the vacant retail spaces.

206621.explanation: The municipality must take the initiative here. The 1950s are not coming back. There are simply too many retail properties from that era.

206623.explanation: This seems something to be addressed regionally.

206624.explanation: Is this the municipality’s task? Following national policies seems more appropriate to us.

206625.explanation: The safety of provincial roads is the province’s responsibility. Fortunately, there have been few accidents at crossings so far. But the province now uses this against us: no accidents, so no need. Proactive action from the municipality could be helpful.

206626.explanation: Access to the hamlet/small village De Haart is downright poor. Widening the Haartseweg only solves part of the problem. The other problem is good connection to the provincial road network. Much improvement is possible here.

206627.explanation: Should be integrated into a comprehensive policy for youth and young adults. The key is to find the right balance between enforcement and care.

206628.explanation: Too simplistic. Many citizens can afford relationship therapy themselves. It is important, though, to also have a social policy safety net here.

206629.explanation: A municipal policy promoting social cohesion and community spirit is useful. The municipality should primarily focus on creating opportunities.

206630.explanation: The question is indeed whether every home needs a parking space as is current policy. Larger parking areas around the centers might be more sensible. This does not alter that accessibility for people with disabilities must be preserved in the centers.

206568.explanation: The objectives are central; funding is needed to achieve them. Lowering the property tax makes necessary expenditures more difficult. With a small increase of twenty euros per year, the municipality can already do a lot. Moreover, the property tax is fair: those who own more contribute more.

206571.explanation: Opening hours are already limited (06:00-22:00). Entrepreneurs should be able to choose if and how long to open on Sundays. No extra restrictions are needed.

206572.explanation: We want to give farmers space for other sources of income than intensive farming, such as a shop or campsite. But it must fit the area. Therefore, we oppose the word “always” in the statement.

206573.explanation: Newcomers must integrate faster and become independent. This requires extra language lessons, education, and work experience. Linking laws and education improves this. Good guidance costs more money but benefits everyone and the labor market.

206574.explanation: We want a healthy, clean living environment. Wood stoves cause harmful substances and fine dust, especially bad for people with lung diseases. Therefore, we want the government to quickly ban wood burning for clean air for all.

206575.explanation: Solar panels provide farmers with extra income and contribute to sustainable energy against climate warming. There are solutions where agriculture and solar panels coexist well. We strongly support this.

206576.explanation: There are already possibilities to build on farmyards, and we allow this to be expanded. Not everywhere and not always. Preferably on the yard, and fitting in the rural environment.

206577.explanation: Swimming is important for youth and sports. Recreational swimming must also be possible. We don’t need to compete with large pools. Whether new construction is necessary remains to be seen. If reusing existing buildings is possible, we prefer that because it is more sustainable.

206579.explanation: We support sports clubs that incur extra costs for athletes with disabilities, youth, and elderly. We also help with the accessibility of sports buildings and investigate whether privatization was good. This keeps sports accessible to everyone.

206580.explanation: The municipality works according to an approved enforcement plan with sufficient staff. Priorities can temporarily shift, for example during fireworks season. This way we ensure a safe neighborhood.

206581.explanation: Neighborhood officers are already easily reachable, also via social media. The new citizen service desk offers 24/7 contact options. The police decide their deployment with national funds. Together we ensure safety.

206582.explanation: Everyone should be able to choose where they live. The housing corporation allocates homes: half by lottery, half by waiting time. We do not participate in “own people first,” but choose a fair system for everyone.

206583.explanation: In Aalten, 50% earn income suitable for social housing, but the share is only 19%. The law aims for 30%, so a catch-up is needed. Everyone has the right to a good home, including those with limited means.

206584.explanation: Space is needed for new homes, but it is scarce due to agriculture, nature, and businesses. Building higher uses less land. That is smart and efficient.

206585.explanation: Holiday homes are meant for tourists and are often unsuitable as permanent residences. To house residents properly, we must provide suitable housing, not convert holiday homes.

206588.explanation: A car is handy for groceries, but for pleasant shopping a car-free center is better. Parking spaces – also for bicycles – near the center are the best solution.

206589.explanation: Electric cars are getting better, cheaper, and available second-hand. More people will switch because it is better for climate and environment. Therefore, sufficient charging points must be provided.

206590.explanation: 30 km/h is much safer for all road users and results in less noise and emissions. The time saved by 50 km/h in built-up areas is minimal, while the risks are significantly greater. Therefore, we choose 30 km/h.

206591.explanation: Debt assistance helps entrepreneurs become and remain debt-free. With advice and financial support (such as Bbz), the business often can continue. This allows entrepreneurs to use their talents and maintain jobs and income.

206593.explanation: We want to start citizens’ assemblies. This increases trust, reduces conflicts, and often leads to better decisions. In 2026, we will investigate how this works best so everyone feels heard.

206595.explanation: It seems social but gives new stores an unfair advantage and can put existing stores in trouble. After the first year, you may actually have fewer stores. That is why we oppose this approach.

206596.explanation: We support companies that work sustainably and provide space for everyone. The municipality sets a good example and applies these requirements to companies that work for us. This way, we choose local businesses and an inclusive labor market.

206600.explanation: Employers are primarily responsible for housing and welfare of staff. We want a central point for questions about language and housing, in collaboration with local organizations such as Figulus Welzijn.

206601.explanation: The local welfare organization Figulus already provides cycling lessons for newcomers at the request of the municipality. Therefore, extra municipal effort is not needed.

206602.explanation: Stricter inspections prevent unfair competition and support companies that follow the rules. Inspections also serve to inform; violations do not always have to be punished. Together, this ensures a better environment.

206603.explanation: We want to improve and simplify permit procedures, not abolish them. A good application helps to clarify everything. Accidents can happen at large parties and events, and we want to prevent those.

206604.explanation: We want to improve and simplify permit applications for everyone, with support where needed. A good application helps to clearly organize everything. Not just for young people, but for everyone.

206605.explanation: Tourism is welcome. However, peace and space must be maintained. Unrestricted growth is not desired. A large holiday park can only be allowed if it fits well into the landscape and is carefully considered.

206606.explanation: Homes of residents with limited means are often poorly insulated, causing high energy costs. Free insulation materials tackle the problem at the source, help instead of an energy allowance, and make the municipality greener.

206607.explanation: The national government already has exit schemes. The municipality does not need to create an additional scheme. It is better to look with farmers at repurposing, such as housing. This way, farmers gain income, home seekers get a place, and the countryside gains livability.

206608.explanation: There is still much uncertainty about agricultural pesticides, but studies point to health risks such as Parkinson’s and cancer. The precautionary principle must apply: avoid risks, especially near homes.

206609.explanation: With taller turbines, Hagenwind can generate up to five times more sustainable energy. Modern technologies limit noise and shadow flicker and protect birds. This should be possible, provided the benefits also serve the local community. There are several options for this.

206610.explanation: We want to preserve existing buildings to ensure sustainability and beauty. But educational quality is important: if new construction is better, the old building can get a new function. The council has decided on new construction of Schaersvoorde. We will not reverse that decision.

206611.explanation: We want sports close to home and in the center. Large sports parks in one place are difficult due to limited space. New sports will be accommodated on existing sites, so everyone can exercise close to home.

206612.explanation: We are making a plan for the entire area around De Ahof and De Pol. A skatepark can be part of that. Until then, the current skatepark remains available so young people can safely exercise outside.

206613.explanation: Many buildings belong to associations themselves; they bear costs and receive income. In case of loss, the municipality can sometimes assist with subsidies. We are investigating whether this system still works well so that sports and culture remain affordable.

206614.explanation: Cells nearby do not increase safety and also cost money and manpower. More important is having enough officers on the street. The police make a careful assessment whether arrest and transfer to a cell is necessary.

206615.explanation: We support youth clubs. Young people learn to cooperate and take responsibility there. They must ensure fire safety themselves. The municipality provides information and help. Subsidies can be applied for, just like others. Together, we ensure a safe place for young people.

206616.explanation: With our own housing company, we can build courtyards, care homes, and rental houses faster. In the coming period, we will further develop this idea. This way, the corporation can fully focus on social rental housing, and together we ensure suitable homes for everyone.

206617.explanation: A strict parking standard hinders new construction and transformation of stores into homes. Not everyone has a car, so customization is needed to maintain space and flexibility.

206618.explanation: We support active land policy: the municipality buys land to prevent speculation and price increases. Part of it can go to elderly people (preferably combined with youth) for communal living forms such as courtyards. We are strong proponents of this.

206619.explanation: Everyone should easily find a toilet. We support the “Hoge Nood” (Big Need) campaign and ask shops and residents to open their toilets. Together with stakeholders, we seek the best solution. Agreed, but not by the municipality.

206620.explanation: Vacancy of homes, shops, and offices must be combated. There is great demand for living space. A fine for prolonged vacancy helps to bring properties back into use.

206621.explanation: Owners are responsible for converting vacant shops and therefore are the first to act.

206623.explanation: Tourists often choose a region, such as the Achterhoek. We already cooperate on promotion and locally there is sufficient PR budget available. Additional municipal advertising is therefore unnecessary.

206624.explanation: We will consult with schools about good education so that children learn to manage money early. We also involve elderly people and people with disabilities through our advisory team and Figulus. This way, everyone gets the right help and information.

206625.explanation: Traffic safety on the N318 is a provincial responsibility. If improvement requires municipal contribution, that is negotiable. Safety comes first, but the primary responsibility lies with the province.

206626.explanation: Wider roads lead to higher speeds and thus more and more serious accidents. We want to prevent that. Therefore, widening is not a good idea.

206627.explanation: We collaborate with organizations such as Iriszorg and Tactus for addiction help. They have the knowledge and resources and must actively reach out to young people. More effort from these organizations ensures a strong approach that truly helps.

206628.explanation: From 2026, help will be easy to find via the Citizens’ Square and the Question & Advice team, such as social work or Wmo. We want to prevent problems by better understanding home situations and thus addressing causes faster.

206629.explanation: Loneliness hinders a happy life. We strengthen the social foundation, make caring for each other normal, and build caring neighborhoods. This way, we ensure that everyone belongs and feels less alone.

206630.explanation: The core shopping areas are developing from shopping districts to recreational areas. This calls for a different design, such as in Dinxperlo. Low-traffic shopping areas provide peace and coziness. This makes the center attractive and accessible.

206568.explanation: Strengthen residents' purchasing power and the business climate.

206571.explanation: It is up to the entrepreneurs and shopkeepers themselves.

206572.explanation: It must be a suitable location.

206573.explanation: The national government is responsible for this and the cost should not be placed on the municipality.

206574.explanation: More communication about the heating guide and how to use a wood stove responsibly.

206575.explanation: First make maximum use of the available roofs.

206576.explanation: Random building of homes on farmland can cause obstacles for farmers. A former farmhouse site can be suitable to build new homes.

206577.explanation: The existing swimming pool 't Walfort must be renovated.

206579.explanation: Primarily, it is the responsibility of the association. The municipality can, however, make locations and accommodations available.

206580.explanation: We have enough BOAs (Public Order Officers), but there is a need for more police on the street.

206581.explanation: Close to the citizen to increase the feeling of safety.

206582.explanation: Especially for people with a socio-economic connection to the municipality.

206583.explanation: In the municipality of Aalten, home ownership is particularly cherished. Young people prefer their own affordable home over a rental property.

206584.explanation: The housing shortage is significant. With limited high-rise buildings, it is possible to realize more affordable homes.

206585.explanation: There is also a need for vacation homes, and recreation is good for our hospitality and retail sectors. Additionally, we must work on a real solution, which is to create affordable housing.

206588.explanation: Encourage that parcel pickup points are placed outside shopping areas. Shopping districts should remain primarily accessible for shoppers and the hospitality industry.

206589.explanation: Only when there is demonstrable demand for more charging points can additional locations be designated.

206590.explanation: Only where it is demonstrably desirable to achieve a safe traffic situation.

206593.explanation: That is what the representatives, the council members, are for. They are democratically elected. Besides, the municipality can already initiate a participation process.

206595.explanation: It is intervening in a free market, which is not desirable.

206596.explanation: The municipality must procure carefully but can also give social enterprises opportunities.

206600.explanation: It is primarily an employer’s responsibility.

206601.explanation: That is a task of the national government.

206602.explanation: If you set rules, you must also comply with them. Preferably, we set fewer rules.

206603.explanation: The organization should submit a notification instead of applying for a permit. The municipality confirms the notification and states the rules that must be followed.

206604.explanation: Age should not play a role. The goal should be to reduce the number of rules.

206605.explanation: It is good for the local economy, and as residents, we benefit from it again.

206606.explanation: Homeowners should be able to apply for subsidies for insulation. The municipality may require landlords to also invest in making rental homes more sustainable.

206607.explanation: The municipality can allow new houses on the farmyard. With the sale of the new homes, the retiring farmer can finance the demolition of the stables.

206608.explanation: This is the responsibility of the central government. They must establish rules for this.

206609.explanation: First, it must be investigated whether taller wind turbines lead to undesirable effects.

206610.explanation: Depends on the school and must be assessed per location.

206611.explanation: The number of volunteers is decreasing, making it increasingly difficult to maintain independent associations. You can also better utilize facilities and add social community functions.

206612.explanation: It must be considered within the overall plan for Ahof and De Pol, combined with the youth’s need for appropriate facilities.

206613.explanation: Paying property tax applies to everyone and everything. Where is the limit when making exceptions? It can be assessed whether associations with a lot of real estate can be partly compensated via a subsidy.

206614.explanation: Detention cells being located remotely (as is now the case) reduces the presence of police officers in the municipality. In case of unrest or accidents, officers have to come from far away.

206615.explanation: It would be desirable for young people to receive information from the fire department. It might also offer an opportunity to recruit new volunteers for the fire department.

206616.explanation: We have the Achterhoek Housing Cooperative with, for example, the Uuthuuskes. Perhaps we can explore with this organization whether they can place more rental homes.

206617.explanation: For each development, it must be assessed whether parking spaces should be added. Additionally, extra parking spots must be created with the help of a parking fund.

206618.explanation: The municipality should remove restrictive rules.

206619.explanation: Assess with a pilot in Dinxperlo whether it will be utilized.

206620.explanation: The VVD values respecting property rights. Only if vacancy leads to significant deterioration of the surroundings and infringes on others’ property rights should the municipality be able to intervene.

206621.explanation: In places where shops are not necessary, the municipality should still offer opportunities for conversion. It is not necessary for the municipality to invest money in this.

206623.explanation: Tourists use the hospitality industry and shops. This extra revenue helps to retain these businesses in the municipality, which is also in the interest of the residents.

206624.explanation: It is not a municipal task.

206625.explanation: If it were an affordable option, it could have been realized. With the same money, other traffic measures can be taken that are more useful in increasing traffic safety.

206626.explanation: Will road users drive even faster and will it become more unsafe on the Haartseweg?

206627.explanation: There is sufficient assistance available. The financial resources can be better spent on education and prevention.

206628.explanation: It is a private matter. The municipality has no role in this.

206629.explanation: There are already enough initiatives. Residents experiencing loneliness should be better referred to the existing initiatives.

206630.explanation: We are not in favor of the retail offering in Aalten and Dinxperlo.

206568.explanation: The ChristenUnie has a cautious policy when it comes to (increasing) municipal taxes. For many people, the cost of daily life is high, and therefore the municipality must take into account the (housing) expenses of our residents.

206571.explanation: Although Sunday shopping is possible in the municipality of Aalten, we continue to uphold the principle of Sunday rest. Therefore, the ChristenUnie is against extending Sunday openings. Shopkeepers should also not be forced to open their stores on Sunday.

206572.explanation: The ChristenUnie sees opportunities for farmers to be active in other areas as well. Therefore, the ChristenUnie encourages allowing diversified agriculture, such as nature management, farm shops, and (mini)campings.

206573.explanation: Integration and civic integration of newcomers is important to the ChristenUnie so that they can be quickly included in society. At the same time, the ChristenUnie sees that our welfare organization (Figulus) already does this well with sufficient resources.

206574.explanation: Although air pollution can cause nuisance and inconvenience, the ChristenUnie believes that it should not be forbidden in the municipality of Aalten. The ChristenUnie does advocate for more information and communication on this topic so that people become aware of possible nuisance and can better manage their heating.

206575.explanation: Agricultural land is valuable for farming use and, as far as the ChristenUnie is concerned, should remain so. However, it should be possible to establish a solar field to a limited extent (e.g., 1 hectare) so that we meet our (national) energy targets.

206576.explanation: There is a huge shortage of housing and at the same time many farmers are struggling, partly due to national regulations. The municipality's role is limited, but where possible the municipality contributes. This can be done by allowing space to build homes on their farms.

206577.explanation: The municipality of Aalten must have sufficient good swimming facilities, both for recreational use and relaxation as well as for sports purposes. This is currently not the case. Therefore, the ChristenUnie believes a new swimming pool should be built at a good and accessible location.

206579.explanation: Sports clubs are very important in our municipality. They contribute to enjoyment, meeting, connection, and health. The municipality has a facilitating and supportive role, also when clubs face financial difficulties.

206580.explanation: Safety in the municipality of Aalten is generally (fortunately) good and should remain that way. The ChristenUnie believes no more money needs to be allocated to enforcement. If that is needed in the future, the ChristenUnie is willing to consider it.

206581.explanation: Safe and livable neighborhoods develop when people look out for each other. The neighborhood police officer as a direct point of contact who is visible and approachable to residents contributes to this.

206582.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants everyone to have their own home, especially residents of the municipality of Aalten. At the same time, other people also want to live in our municipality, and space is needed for that. Therefore, it is very important that more social rental homes become available quickly.

206583.explanation: The ChristenUnie advocates for higher percentages of social rental and affordable owner-occupied homes than currently is the case. Specifically, we want 28% of new-build homes to be social rental homes.

206584.explanation: Residential buildings with multiple floors can contribute to smart space use. New construction must fit the character and scale of the municipality of Aalten. According to the ChristenUnie, buildings of five stories do not fit our villages.

206585.explanation: The ChristenUnie is against generally allowing permanent residence in holiday homes. In special situations, customization is possible, but the principle is that holiday parks retain their recreational function. Exceptions must, therefore, be possible with consideration for human scale.

206588.explanation: The ChristenUnie encourages walking and cycling but is also realistic. For many people, a car remains necessary, for example to do groceries. Therefore, shopping areas in Aalten must also remain easily accessible by car.

206589.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants to expand the number of charging points in the municipality of Aalten. There are already many electric cars, and this also enables further growth.

206590.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants the maximum speed limit within built-up areas to be 30 kilometers per hour in principle. This contributes to traffic safety in our municipality. The municipal council has already decided this, so we want to maintain this policy.

206591.explanation: Entrepreneurs should basically be able to receive debt counseling so that they can continue their business where possible and become financially healthy again. This requires clear conditions, agreements, and guidance.

206593.explanation: The ChristenUnie thinks it is good that people are asked to advise on certain subjects to hear society's views. However, we believe that lottery is not the best way to assemble this group. Also, the ChristenUnie believes that decision-making ultimately rests with the mayor and aldermen and the municipal council.

206595.explanation: The ChristenUnie considers this a good plan if it helps shopkeepers start a store. It is important for livability and attractiveness that the municipality has vital and vibrant shopping centers.

206596.explanation: By doing this, the municipality can serve as an example and help social enterprises. This achieves social goals, which can be greater than the savings from direct cost reduction.

206600.explanation: Participation in society is important for everyone, including labor migrants. The municipality can play a role by offering them a language course.

206601.explanation: Our welfare organization (Figulus) currently does this, which the ChristenUnie approves of. This benefits everyone's traffic safety.

206602.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes that the control and enforcement of environmental regulations is already done sufficiently.

206603.explanation: Although it is good to give space and trust to society, it is wise for the municipality to make agreements with organizers about duration, noise, safety, alcohol use, and possible nuisance.

206604.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes that issuing permits must be easy and accessible for everyone. This is not tied to age.

206605.explanation: A holiday park can be very valuable for (attracting) recreation, tourism, and hospitality. It is important that the park fits the character of our municipality, but according to the ChristenUnie, a maximum number of homes is not necessary.

206606.explanation: Making homes more sustainable is an important and huge task. This contributes to the climate and the affordability of energy. Especially for people with less financial means, this is hard to achieve. The ChristenUnie therefore supports assisting this group.

206607.explanation: Farmers should have the space to demolish vacant barns and, for example, have the possibility to build homes there. The municipality, according to the ChristenUnie, does not need to contribute financially to barn demolition.

206608.explanation: Pesticides are no longer used everywhere, for a healthy and biodiverse living environment. We choose broad pesticide-free zones next to homes, schools, childcare, and care institutions instead of a general ban.

206609.explanation: We have a collective task to achieve the energy objective. Taller wind turbines contribute to this. However, the ChristenUnie believes the maximum height as established by the council is decisive. Participation is also very important, and we want local residents to financially benefit.

206610.explanation: Many school buildings in the municipality of Aalten are (heavily) outdated. This requires new construction or renovation. In some cases, it is better to build new so that it better aligns with their educational vision and the buildings last for a long time.

206611.explanation: Having multifunctional sports parks can have advantages for multiple parties regarding space use and volunteer deployment. However, for the ChristenUnie, it is not an objective that all facilities are brought together.

206612.explanation: The skatepark in Aalten is outdated and dilapidated. Young people want a pleasant and green meeting place. Therefore, the current skatepark location should be improved to create a nice and safe place for them for play and meeting.

206613.explanation: The principle that the municipality of Aalten levies property tax on property owners is supported by the ChristenUnie. In cases where certain associations face financial challenges, the municipality should be willing to financially support and accommodate them.

206614.explanation: The loss of the detention complex in Doetinchem is a painful decision by the National Police. The Christian Union would like to see a detention complex located nearby again, so that police officers need less time and are more visible in the municipality of Aalten.

206615.explanation: Our youth in the municipality of Aalten often gather in hangouts and dens. To ensure these are also safe meeting places, it is good that the municipality makes them aware of safety and health in and around these spots.

206616.explanation: There is already a housing corporation active in the municipality of Aalten responsible for this. The municipality must ensure that good agreements are made with this party so that they genuinely realize enough rental housing in our municipality.

206617.explanation: The Christian Union believes that not every new house needs a mandatory parking space. Too strict rules should not hinder housing development. A suitable solution should be sought for each situation.

206618.explanation: Our municipality is aging and there are too few suitable homes for the elderly. The Christian Union therefore supports making places available where, for example, elderly courtyards can be realized, more than there currently are.

206619.explanation: The Christian Union believes that everyone should feel welcome in Aalten. That is why good basic facilities are important. Especially the elderly, families, and people with disabilities benefit from this. Therefore, the Christian Union wants public toilets to be established in the municipality of Aalten.

206620.explanation: The Christian Union finds it important that homes do not stand empty for (too) long. It is important to engage in dialogue with owners to find a solution. Only if that does not work should a fine be considered.

206621.explanation: The Christian Union believes that some vacant shops can be converted into homes, or parts thereof. This helps keep village centers livable and lively. A good mix of shops and housing can be helpful in that regard. In certain places, this can work well together.

206623.explanation: Fortunately, much attention is already being paid to recreation and tourism in the Achterhoek context (including through Achterhoek Promotion), such as walking, cycling, and local activities.

206624.explanation: The Christian Union Aalten finds it important that secondary school students learn how to manage money. It is important that the municipality of Aalten — together with education — offers lessons on this, so that young people better learn to save, spend, and plan their money.

206625.explanation: The Christian Union believes that traffic is (very) dangerous at certain spots on the N318, such as crossing near Lintelo and Aalten. Measures must be taken quickly to make the road and crossings safer for everyone who drives, cycles, or walks there.

206626.explanation: The Christian Union disagrees with widening the Haartseweg. The road can remain as it is. Other solutions can be sought to regulate traffic safely and properly.

206627.explanation: The Christian Union thinks that our welfare organization (Figulus) is doing good work. They have a good overview of vulnerable and/or problematic youth. If they indicate that extra help is needed, the Christian Union would like to listen and explore what is possible.

206628.explanation: Many children in youth care come from divorced families, which can cause problems. The Christian Union wants to help by offering advice and support regarding relationship problems and divorces, for example, through a service point or affordable relationship therapy, so that children are less affected.

206629.explanation: The Christian Union Aalten agrees with this statement. The municipality must and can do more to reduce loneliness, for example by organizing activities, encouraging neighborhood contacts, and supporting people who feel lonely. The municipality must partner with other organizations on this.

206630.explanation: The Christian Union first wants to investigate whether (partially) making the centers car-free is desirable and contributes to the safety of pedestrians and cyclists.

statements.title: In the municipality of Aalten residential buildings of five storeys and more may be permitted.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
Aalten must have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality must attract shops to shopping centers by paying their rent for the first year.
The municipality must no longer designate parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.
People who are new to the Netherlands.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
A group of residents selected by lottery should advise the municipality on important issues.
The Hagenwind wind turbines must be replaced by taller turbines that produce more energy. The municipality should urge the owners to do this.

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.
These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

titleParts.text: die meer energie opleveren. Daar moet de gemeente bij de eigenaren op aandringen.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality of Aalten must more strictly check whether companies comply with environmental regulations.
The municipality must ask the police to hold a weekly meeting with the community police officer in each village.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de politie vragen om in elk dorp wekelijks een

titleParts.glossary: A fixed time when residents can ask questions or drop in.

statements.title: The municipality of Aalten must contribute to funding a bicycle tunnel or bridge on the N318 at Halteweg.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality must do more to reduce loneliness among its residents.
The municipality must do more advertising to attract tourists to Aalten.
Low-income residents must be given insulation materials for free by the municipality.

titleParts.text: Inwoners met een laag inkomen moeten gratis

titleParts.glossary: Material in roofs, walls and floors that keeps heat in or out. In winter it keeps heat inside and in summer outside.

statements.title: Football pitches, tennis courts and other sports facilities should be brought together in one location as much as possible.

titleParts.glossary: Buildings or services needed for daily life, such as roads, sports facilities or healthcare.

titleParts.text: om te sporten moeten zo veel mogelijk op één plaats bij elkaar worden gebracht.

statements.title: There must be public toilets in Aalten and Dinxperlo.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Aalten must procure as much as possible from social enterprises.

titleParts.text: Aalten moet zoveel mogelijk inkopen bij

titleParts.glossary: Companies that primarily aim to do something good for society. Making a profit comes second.

statements.title: The municipality must exempt sports clubs and cultural associations from property tax (OZB).

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet sportverenigingen en culturele verenigingen geen

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality must contribute to the cost of couples therapy.

titleParts.glossary: When people try to improve their romantic relationship with the help of someone else.

statements.title: A parking space must be available for every new home.
The municipality must invest money in converting empty retail units into homes.
The municipality must ask the police to ensure there are nearby detention cells where arrestees can be held.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de politie vragen om te zorgen voor cellen in de buurt waar

titleParts.glossary: People who have been taken by the police and detained.

statements.title: The municipality should provide lessons on managing money in secondary schools.
The Haartseweg should be widened.
The shopping areas in the municipality of Aalten should be made low-traffic; cars should be parked on the outskirts.

titleParts.text: De winkelgebieden in de gemeente Aalten moeten

titleParts.glossary: That there is little car traffic.

titleParts.text: worden. Auto's parkeer je dan aan de rand.

statements.title: The municipality should make it easier and cheaper for young people up to 25 to obtain permits for events.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet jongeren tot 25 jaar makkelijker en goedkoper een

titleParts.glossary: Permission from the municipality to do something.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality must offer migrant workers a free language course.
The skatepark in Aalten must be refurbished.
Entrepreneurs must be able to apply for debt counseling while keeping their business.
The municipality must offer newcomers free bicycle lessons.
The municipality must restrict Sunday opening hours for shops.
Festivals in Aalten must be permit-free from now on, for example the Oranjefeest and the Schuttersfeest.
The municipality must pay for smoke detectors and fire extinguishers for the units, sheds and caravans where young people gather.
The shopping areas in Aalten must remain easily accessible by car.
The municipality must not build a new swimming pool in Aalten.
The municipality of Aalten must establish its own housing corporation to build rental homes for its residents.
The municipality must not build new schools, but must renovate the existing buildings.
No large holiday park (more than 100 holiday bungalows) may be built in Aalten.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.
The municipality must pay farmers to demolish vacant barns.
The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves must remain permitted in the municipality of Aalten.
Of the new homes to be built in the municipality of Aalten, at least 40% must be social rental housing. Currently 19% of homes in Aalten are social rental housing.
The municipality must make land available to groups of older people who want to build homes together.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.
The municipality should ban the use of agricultural pesticides near homes.
Owners who leave a house vacant for more than a year must receive a fine.
The municipality must hire more youth workers to help young people with addictions.
